texts.label: text
texts.orig: The CPRS Read-Only (CPRS) enables you to select a patient and view reports from the patient’s medical record. As the name implies, you cannot enter, or update the information connected with any patient. The purpose of the CPRS Read-Only is to give controlled access to specific users that will enable them to review the medical records but not change any information.
texts.text: The CPRS Read-Only (CPRS) enables you to select a patient and view reports from the patient’s medical record. As the name implies, you cannot enter, or update the information connected with any patient. The purpose of the CPRS Read-Only is to give controlled access to specific users that will enable them to review the medical records but not change any information.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Users will be able to open the patient charts made available to them, including remote data from other facilities, and view, copy, and print reports that are available from the Reports tab only of the CPRS GUI.
texts.text: Users will be able to open the patient charts made available to them, including remote data from other facilities, and view, copy, and print reports that are available from the Reports tab only of the CPRS GUI.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Instructions, procedures, and other information are available from the CPRS online help file. You may access the help file by clicking on
texts.text: Instructions, procedures, and other information are available from the CPRS online help file. You may access the help file by clicking on

texts.label: text
texts.orig: from the menu bar or by pressing the F1 key while you have any CPRS dialog open. Once the Help is open, use the contents section or the index section to find the instructions or explanation you need.
texts.text: from the menu bar or by pressing the F1 key while you have any CPRS dialog open. Once the Help is open, use the contents section or the index section to find the instructions or explanation you need.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Much of the information in this User Manual is also in the CPRS online help.
texts.text: Much of the information in this User Manual is also in the CPRS online help.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: CPRS was designed to run in both the Microsoft Windows operating environment and on text-based terminals. The terminal or text-based version of CPRS (also known as the List Manager version) is not described in this manual. This manual specifically describes CPRS Read-Only that runs on the Windows operating system.
texts.text: CPRS was designed to run in both the Microsoft Windows operating environment and on text-based terminals. The terminal or text-based version of CPRS (also known as the List Manager version) is not described in this manual. This manual specifically describes CPRS Read-Only that runs on the Windows operating system.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This manual is organized in the way most people will use the CPRS GUI. It begins with how to log on to the system and then how to select a patient. The manual continues with an explanation of the features that are available from the Reports tab.
texts.text: This manual is organized in the way most people will use the CPRS GUI. It begins with how to log on to the system and then how to select a patient. The manual continues with an explanation of the features that are available from the Reports tab.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: We hope this organization will help you understand the basic layout of the CPRS GUI and provide you with information about the specific tasks you will perform.
texts.text: We hope this organization will help you understand the basic layout of the CPRS GUI and provide you with information about the specific tasks you will perform.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Once the CPRS Read-Only application has been installed on your workstation and you have been issued an access code and a verify code, you can sign onto CPRS.
texts.text: Once the CPRS Read-Only application has been installed on your workstation and you have been issued an access code and a verify code, you can sign onto CPRS.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: To start CPRS, use the following steps:
texts.text: To start CPRS, use the following steps:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Double-click on the CPRS icon on your desktop.
texts.text: Double-click on the CPRS icon on your desktop.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: If the Connect To dialog appears, click on the down-arrow, select the appropriate account (if more than one exists), and click
texts.text: If the Connect To dialog appears, click on the down-arrow, select the appropriate account (if more than one exists), and click

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The VISTA logo window opens for a few moments and is followed by the  Sign-on dialog as shown below.
texts.text: The VISTA logo window opens for a few moments and is followed by the  Sign-on dialog as shown below.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Type your access code into the Access Code field and press the
texts.text: Type your access code into the Access Code field and press the

texts.label: text
texts.orig: key. Then, type your verify code into the Verify Code field and press the
texts.text: key. Then, type your verify code into the Verify Code field and press the

texts.label: text
texts.orig: You can also type the access code, followed by a semicolon, followed by the verify code. Once you have done this press the
texts.text: You can also type the access code, followed by a semicolon, followed by the verify code. Once you have done this press the

texts.label: text
texts.orig: CPRS will then bring up the Patient Selection screen.
texts.text: CPRS will then bring up the Patient Selection screen.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: After you log in to CPRS, the Patient Selection screen, shown below, is the first thing to appear. You should now select a patient record to view.
texts.text: After you log in to CPRS, the Patient Selection screen, shown below, is the first thing to appear. You should now select a patient record to view.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: To select a patient record, follow these steps:
texts.text: To select a patient record, follow these steps:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: If you just logged into CPRS, skip to step 2. If you have been viewing another record, select
texts.text: If you just logged into CPRS, skip to step 2. If you have been viewing another record, select

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Type the first letter of the patient’s last name and the last four digits of the patient’s Social Security number (s0001).
texts.text: Type the first letter of the patient’s last name and the last four digits of the patient’s Social Security number (s0001).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Type the last four numbers of the patient’s social security number (0001) or type the last four numbers of the social security number with “P” as the last character (0002p) if your facility uses special codes for those patients who do not have a social security number or it is unknown.
texts.text: Type the last four numbers of the patient’s social security number (0001) or type the last four numbers of the social security number with “P” as the last character (0002p) if your facility uses special codes for those patients who do not have a social security number or it is unknown.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Type part or all of the patient’s name (e.g., “patien” or “patient,one”).
texts.text: Type part or all of the patient’s name (e.g., “patien” or “patient,one”).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: CPRS will try to match what you entered to a patient and highlight that patient. The highlighted patient's name and other demographic information will appear below the Cancel button.
texts.text: CPRS will try to match what you entered to a patient and highlight that patient. The highlighted patient's name and other demographic information will appear below the Cancel button.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Verify that the correct patient is highlighted. If the correct patient is highlighted, click
texts.text: Verify that the correct patient is highlighted. If the correct patient is highlighted, click

texts.label: text
texts.orig: . If the correct patient is not highlighted, scroll through to find the correct patient, highlight the name, and then click
texts.text: . If the correct patient is not highlighted, scroll through to find the correct patient, highlight the name, and then click

texts.label: text
texts.orig: When you click OK, CPRS opens to the Reports tab as shown in the following capture.
texts.text: When you click OK, CPRS opens to the Reports tab as shown in the following capture.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: After selecting a patient, users will see CPRS open to the Reports tab. The header bar and menu are also present.
texts.text: After selecting a patient, users will see CPRS open to the Reports tab. The header bar and menu are also present.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Features Available from the Header Bar
texts.text: Features Available from the Header Bar

texts.label: text
texts.orig: There are six buttons located at the top of the CPRS window that are available from any tab. These buttons are: the Patient Inquiry button, the Encounter Provider and Location button, the Primary Care button, the Remote Data Views button, the Reminders button, and the Postings (CWAD) button. A detailed explanation of each of these buttons is included below.
texts.text: There are six buttons located at the top of the CPRS window that are available from any tab. These buttons are: the Patient Inquiry button, the Encounter Provider and Location button, the Primary Care button, the Remote Data Views button, the Reminders button, and the Postings (CWAD) button. A detailed explanation of each of these buttons is included below.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Buttons available from any CPRS tab
texts.text: Buttons available from any CPRS tab

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Patient Inquiry button is located on the left side of the chart directly below the menu bar.  The Patient Inquiry button displays the following information:
texts.text: The Patient Inquiry button is located on the left side of the chart directly below the menu bar.  The Patient Inquiry button displays the following information:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Social Security number (or identification number if assigned by the site)
texts.text: Social Security number (or identification number if assigned by the site)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: If you click on the Patient Inquiry button, the Patient Inquiry dialog appears. The Patient Inquiry dialog includes additional information such as the patient’s mailing address, telephone number(s), admission information, and other relevant data. While in the detailed display, you can select a new patient, print the detailed display, or close the detailed display.
texts.text: If you click on the Patient Inquiry button, the Patient Inquiry dialog appears. The Patient Inquiry dialog includes additional information such as the patient’s mailing address, telephone number(s), admission information, and other relevant data. While in the detailed display, you can select a new patient, print the detailed display, or close the detailed display.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: CPRS shows the encounter provider and location for the visit on the Visit Encounter button. You can access this feature from any chart tab.
texts.text: CPRS shows the encounter provider and location for the visit on the Visit Encounter button. You can access this feature from any chart tab.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: To the immediate right of the Visit Encounter button is the Primary Care button, which displays the primary care team and attending physician assigned to this patient. The message “Primary Care Team Unassigned” is displayed if a primary care team has not been assigned.
texts.text: To the immediate right of the Visit Encounter button is the Primary Care button, which displays the primary care team and attending physician assigned to this patient. The message “Primary Care Team Unassigned” is displayed if a primary care team has not been assigned.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: For more information on the attending physician or the primary care team if one has been assigned, click the Primary Care button.
texts.text: For more information on the attending physician or the primary care team if one has been assigned, click the Primary Care button.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: CPRS Read-Only includes the ability to view Clinical Reminders. Reminders are used to aid physicians in performing tasks to fulfill Clinical Practice Guidelines and periodic procedures or education as needed for veteran patients.
texts.text: CPRS Read-Only includes the ability to view Clinical Reminders. Reminders are used to aid physicians in performing tasks to fulfill Clinical Practice Guidelines and periodic procedures or education as needed for veteran patients.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: For more detailed information on Reminders, refer to the
texts.text: For more detailed information on Reminders, refer to the

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Reminders button highlighted in red below shows you at a glance whether the patient has reminders that are due.
texts.text: The Reminders button highlighted in red below shows you at a glance whether the patient has reminders that are due.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: By observing the color and design of the icon on the Reminders button, the user receives immediate feedback on the most important types of Reminders available for the selected patient. Clinical Coordinators can set Reminders to be evaluated when you open the chart or they can set it to evaluate the Reminders only after you click the Reminders button.
texts.text: By observing the color and design of the icon on the Reminders button, the user receives immediate feedback on the most important types of Reminders available for the selected patient. Clinical Coordinators can set Reminders to be evaluated when you open the chart or they can set it to evaluate the Reminders only after you click the Reminders button.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The following icons could be visible on the Reminders button:
texts.text: The following icons could be visible on the Reminders button:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Due: The patient meets all the conditions for the reminder and the appropriate amount of time has elapsed.
texts.text: Due: The patient meets all the conditions for the reminder and the appropriate amount of time has elapsed.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Applicable: The patient meets all the conditions for the reminder, but the appropriate time has not elapsed. For example, a flu shot is given once a year, but it has not been a year yet.
texts.text: Applicable: The patient meets all the conditions for the reminder, but the appropriate time has not elapsed. For example, a flu shot is given once a year, but it has not been a year yet.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Other: Reminders have been defined, but were not specifically evaluated for the selected patient. An important education topic might be placed in Other.
texts.text: Other: Reminders have been defined, but were not specifically evaluated for the selected patient. An important education topic might be placed in Other.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Question Mark: A question mark on the Reminders button indicates that the reminders have not yet been evaluated. This appears when the patient’s chart is first opened to a tab other than the Cover Sheet. Click the Reminders button or the Reminders drawer on the Notes tab to evaluate the reminders.
texts.text: Question Mark: A question mark on the Reminders button indicates that the reminders have not yet been evaluated. This appears when the patient’s chart is first opened to a tab other than the Cover Sheet. Click the Reminders button or the Reminders drawer on the Notes tab to evaluate the reminders.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Grayed-out Alarm Clock: This icon indicates that there are no due nor applicable reminders, nor are there any reminder categories available.
texts.text: Grayed-out Alarm Clock: This icon indicates that there are no due nor applicable reminders, nor are there any reminder categories available.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: If you click the button, you will see a tree view of the patient’s reminders such as the one shown below. The icons that appear on the Reminders button are also used in the tree view to identify the various types of reminders.
texts.text: If you click the button, you will see a tree view of the patient’s reminders such as the one shown below. The icons that appear on the Reminders button are also used in the tree view to identify the various types of reminders.

texts.label: text
texts.orig:
The Available Reminders tree view

The Reminders tab on the Icon Legends dialog includes a description of the different icons that appear on the Reminders tree view. To access the Icon Legend, click

texts.label: text
texts.orig: There is additional information later in this manual about how to retrieve, view, copy, and print remote patient data.
texts.text: There is additional information later in this manual about how to retrieve, view, copy, and print remote patient data.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Postings are special types of progress notes. They contain critical information about a patient that hospital staff need to be aware of.
texts.text: Postings are special types of progress notes. They contain critical information about a patient that hospital staff need to be aware of.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: If a patient record contains postings, the Postings button (located in the upper right corner of the CPRS window) will display the letters C, W, A, and/or D. These letters correspond to the four types of postings described below.
texts.text: If a patient record contains postings, the Postings button (located in the upper right corner of the CPRS window) will display the letters C, W, A, and/or D. These letters correspond to the four types of postings described below.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: ) – Cautionary information about critical behavior or health of a patient.
texts.text: ) – Cautionary information about critical behavior or health of a patient.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: ) – Notifications that inform medical center staff about possible risks associated with a patient.
texts.text: ) – Notifications that inform medical center staff about possible risks associated with a patient.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: ) – Posting that includes information about medications, foods, and other conditions to which the patient is allergic or may have an adverse reaction.
texts.text: ) – Posting that includes information about medications, foods, and other conditions to which the patient is allergic or may have an adverse reaction.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Example: Patient allergic to penicillin and latex.
texts.text: Example: Patient allergic to penicillin and latex.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: ) – Also called advanced directives, directives are recorded agreements that a patient and/or family have made with the clinical staff.
texts.text: ) – Also called advanced directives, directives are recorded agreements that a patient and/or family have made with the clinical staff.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Example: DNR (Do Not Resuscitate) directive on file.
texts.text: Example: DNR (Do Not Resuscitate) directive on file.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: You can view a posting by pressing the Postings button.
texts.text: You can view a posting by pressing the Postings button.

texts.orig: To view a posting by using the Postings button, follow these steps:
texts.text: To view a posting by using the Postings button, follow these steps:

button.
The Patient Postings dialog appears. The Patient Postings dialog contains all postings for the selected patient. The postings are divided into two categories. Allergies are listed in the top half of the dialog and crisis notes, warning notes, and directives are listed in the bottom half.

texts.text: View remote data for the patient from other VA Medical Centers (VAMCs) and/or Department of Defense data if available (To learn more about remote data, go to the Remote Data section of this manual.)

texts.orig: To set up the reports options, select Tools | Options and then follow the steps below.
texts.text: To set up the reports options, select Tools | Options and then follow the steps below.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This tab allows you to set the date ranges and the maximum number of occurrences for CPRS reports. You can change the settings for all reports or for individual reports.
texts.text: This tab allows you to set the date ranges and the maximum number of occurrences for CPRS reports. You can change the settings for all reports or for individual reports.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: This option allows you to set a start date, a stop date, and a maximum number of occurrences for all CPRS reports. After you click the Set All Reports… button, the “Change Default Settings For Available CPRS Reports” dialog will appear.
texts.text: This option allows you to set a start date, a stop date, and a maximum number of occurrences for all CPRS reports. After you click the Set All Reports… button, the “Change Default Settings For Available CPRS Reports” dialog will appear.

texts.text: The Change Default Setting For Available CPRS Reports dialog

a) entering a date (e.g. 6/21/01 or June 21, 2001).
b) entering a date formula (e.g. t-200).
c) pressing the  button to bring up a calendar.

texts.text: Available Reports on the Reports Tab

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The table below lists the reports available from the Reports tab. A “+” sign indicates that the topic is a heading that can be expanded. Some of these reports may have remote data.
texts.text: The table below lists the reports available from the Reports tab. A “+” sign indicates that the topic is a heading that can be expanded. Some of these reports may have remote data.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In the list below, those reports that may have remote data from the Department of Defense are noted. Also, there is a part of the tree that lists the Department of Defense reports. Please note that the order of the reports may be different depending on the configuration of your site. Below is the exported list from CPRS.
texts.text: In the list below, those reports that may have remote data from the Department of Defense are noted. Also, there is a part of the tree that lists the Department of Defense reports. Please note that the order of the reports may be different depending on the configuration of your site. Below is the exported list from CPRS.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: If a report is available in a table view, the table can be sorted alphabetically, numerically, or by date. To sort data in a report table:
texts.text: If a report is available in a table view, the table can be sorted alphabetically, numerically, or by date. To sort data in a report table:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Click the column heading to sort by.
texts.text: Click the column heading to sort by.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The table will be sorted alphabetically (A-Z), numerically (0-9), or by date (most recent-least recent).
texts.text: The table will be sorted alphabetically (A-Z), numerically (0-9), or by date (most recent-least recent).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: If you click the column heading again, the table will be sorted in inverse order (Z-A, 9-0, or least recent-most recent).
texts.text: If you click the column heading again, the table will be sorted in inverse order (Z-A, 9-0, or least recent-most recent).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: To perform a secondary sort, click on another column heading.
texts.text: To perform a secondary sort, click on another column heading.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Note: If you hold the pointer over the table, a hover hint will appear with the criteria used to sort the table.
texts.text: Note: If you hold the pointer over the table, a hover hint will appear with the criteria used to sort the table.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The reports tab shown with a table view that can be sorted by clicking the column headings.
texts.text: The reports tab shown with a table view that can be sorted by clicking the column headings.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: To print a report, follow these steps:
texts.text: To print a report, follow these steps:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: From the Reports tab, select the report to print.
texts.text: From the Reports tab, select the report to print.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: If the report is in text format, right-click on the text of the report
texts.text: If the report is in text format, right-click on the text of the report

texts.label: text
texts.orig: if the report is in table format, click on the row that contains the data to print (to select more than one row, press and hold either the
texts.text: if the report is in table format, click on the row that contains the data to print (to select more than one row, press and hold either the

texts.label: text
texts.orig: key). After selecting the appropriate row(s), right-click on the area or row you have selected.
texts.text: key). After selecting the appropriate row(s), right-click on the area or row you have selected.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This capture shows the pop-up menu that appears when you right-click on an entry.
texts.text: This capture shows the pop-up menu that appears when you right-click on an entry.

texts.label: text
texts.orig:
If a printer has not been selected as the default, select a printer from the dialog that displays, as shown below.
The dialog box shown below comes up when you right click on a report and select Print
texts.text:
If a printer has not been selected as the default, select a printer from the dialog that displays, as shown below.
The dialog box shown below comes up when you right click on a report and select Print

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Normally, you do not need to enter a right margin or page length value. These values are measured in characters and normally are already defined by the device.
texts.text: Normally, you do not need to enter a right margin or page length value. These values are measured in characters and normally are already defined by the device.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: To copy data from a report, follow these steps:
texts.text: To copy data from a report, follow these steps:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: From the Reports tab, select the report to copy data from.
texts.text: From the Reports tab, select the report to copy data from.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: If the report is in text format, select the text to copy and then right-click
texts.text: If the report is in text format, select the text to copy and then right-click

texts.label: text
texts.orig: if the report is in table format, click on the row that contains the data to copy (to select more than one row, press and hold either the
texts.text: if the report is in table format, click on the row that contains the data to copy (to select more than one row, press and hold either the

texts.label: text
texts.orig: key). After selecting the appropriate rows, right-click on the area or row you have selected.
texts.text: key). After selecting the appropriate rows, right-click on the area or row you have selected.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: You can now paste the data into another area in CPRS or into another program.
texts.text: You can now paste the data into another area in CPRS or into another program.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: To display a Health Summary, follow these steps:
texts.text: To display a Health Summary, follow these steps:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Select a patient after you enter the CPRS system.
texts.text: Select a patient after you enter the CPRS system.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Under the Available Reports box on the left side of the screen, click the “+” sign in order to expand the Health Summary heading.
texts.text: Under the Available Reports box on the left side of the screen, click the “+” sign in order to expand the Health Summary heading.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Select a Health Summary by clicking on the summary that you would like to see.  After selecting a summary, the appropriate data is displayed on the right side of the screen.
texts.text: Select a Health Summary by clicking on the summary that you would like to see.  After selecting a summary, the appropriate data is displayed on the right side of the screen.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Use the scroll bar on the right to scroll through the different sections of the Health Summary.
texts.text: Use the scroll bar on the right to scroll through the different sections of the Health Summary.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: You can view remote patient data with CPRS if Master Patient Index/Patient Demographics (MPI/PD) and the appropriate patches have been installed at your site. If these patches have been installed and the proper parameters have been set, you can access remote data from other VA Medical Centers (VAMCs) and Department of Defense (DoD) facilities.
texts.text: You can view remote patient data with CPRS if Master Patient Index/Patient Demographics (MPI/PD) and the appropriate patches have been installed at your site. If these patches have been installed and the proper parameters have been set, you can access remote data from other VA Medical Centers (VAMCs) and Department of Defense (DoD) facilities.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: How Do I Know a Patient Has Remote Medical Data
texts.text: How Do I Know a Patient Has Remote Medical Data

texts.label: text
texts.orig: As part of opening a patient record, CPRS checks in the Treating Facility file to see if the selected patient has been seen in other facilities. If the patient has remote data, the words on the Remote Data button turn blue as shown in the image below. If there is no remote data for the selected patient, the letters are gray.
texts.text: As part of opening a patient record, CPRS checks in the Treating Facility file to see if the selected patient has been seen in other facilities. If the patient has remote data, the words on the Remote Data button turn blue as shown in the image below. If there is no remote data for the selected patient, the letters are gray.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: What Does the List of Sites Represent?
texts.text: What Does the List of Sites Represent?

texts.label: text
texts.orig: If you click on the Remote Data button, a drop-down list appears with the name(s) of sites where the patient has been seen. This list is based on either:
texts.text: If you click on the Remote Data button, a drop-down list appears with the name(s) of sites where the patient has been seen. This list is based on either:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Sites that have been specifically designated for your facility to access. These sites are assigned in a parameter that your Clinical Applications Coordinator (CAC) can set up.
texts.text: Sites that have been specifically designated for your facility to access. These sites are assigned in a parameter that your Clinical Applications Coordinator (CAC) can set up.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: All VAMC sites where the patient has been seen and Department of Defense remote data if it is available.
texts.text: All VAMC sites where the patient has been seen and Department of Defense remote data if it is available.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: What Kind of Data Can I View?
texts.text: What Kind of Data Can I View?

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Currently with CPRS Read-Only , you can view Health Summary components and reports available on the Reports tab. There are limitations to what you can view. You can view health summary components that have the same name on both the local and the remote site. You can therefore exchange national Health Summaries, but locally defined components may not be available unless the other site also has a component with the same name.
texts.text: Currently with CPRS Read-Only , you can view Health Summary components and reports available on the Reports tab. There are limitations to what you can view. You can view health summary components that have the same name on both the local and the remote site. You can therefore exchange national Health Summaries, but locally defined components may not be available unless the other site also has a component with the same name.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: If it is available, CPRS can also show some Department of Defense remote data.
texts.text: If it is available, CPRS can also show some Department of Defense remote data.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: How Will the Remote Data Be Viewed?
texts.text: How Will the Remote Data Be Viewed?

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Viewing remote data is a two-step process. First, you select which remote sites to view data from, and then select the specific information you want to view, such as Health Summary components.
texts.text: Viewing remote data is a two-step process. First, you select which remote sites to view data from, and then select the specific information you want to view, such as Health Summary components.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: On the Reports tab, each site you select will have a separate tab for its data. Using the above graphic as an example, you would see six tabs on the Reports tab: Local, Dept. of Defense, Mars, Pluto, Uranus, and Venus.
texts.text: On the Reports tab, each site you select will have a separate tab for its data. Using the above graphic as an example, you would see six tabs on the Reports tab: Local, Dept. of Defense, Mars, Pluto, Uranus, and Venus.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: You would then select the reports you want to view and a date range (if necessary). After this, CPRS will attempt to retrieve those reports if they are available on the remote sites. You would then click on each Treatment Facility’s tab to see the report from that site. While CPRS is attempting to retrieve the data, the message “Transmission in Progress:” is displayed until the data is retrieved.
texts.text: You would then select the reports you want to view and a date range (if necessary). After this, CPRS will attempt to retrieve those reports if they are available on the remote sites. You would then click on each Treatment Facility’s tab to see the report from that site. While CPRS is attempting to retrieve the data, the message “Transmission in Progress:” is displayed until the data is retrieved.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: To view a patient’s remote data, use these steps
texts.text: To view a patient’s remote data, use these steps

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: After opening the patient's record, see if the text on the Remote Data button is blue. If the text is blue, the patient has remote data.
texts.text: After opening the patient's record, see if the text on the Remote Data button is blue. If the text is blue, the patient has remote data.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: button to display a list of sites that have remote data for the selected patient.
texts.text: button to display a list of sites that have remote data for the selected patient.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: if you want data from all the sites listed, or click the check box in front of the site names you want to view remote data from and close the Remote Data button by clicking the button again.
texts.text: if you want data from all the sites listed, or click the check box in front of the site names you want to view remote data from and close the Remote Data button by clicking the button again.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:
Select the report you would like to view from the Available Reports section on the left side of the screen (click the “+” sign in order to expand a report heading).

It may take a few minutes to retrieve the data. While CPRS retrieves the data, the message "Transmission in Progress" is displayed.
texts.text:
Select the report you would like to view from the Available Reports section on the left side of the screen (click the “+” sign in order to expand a report heading).

It may take a few minutes to retrieve the data. While CPRS retrieves the data, the message "Transmission in Progress" is displayed.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Depending on how the report or lab is configured, CPRS will return the remote data in one of two ways as shown below.
texts.text: Depending on how the report or lab is configured, CPRS will return the remote data in one of two ways as shown below.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: If the remote data is in text format, the data from each remote site will be displayed under a separate site tab. To view data from a particular site, click on the appropriate tab.
texts.text: If the remote data is in text format, the data from each remote site will be displayed under a separate site tab. To view data from a particular site, click on the appropriate tab.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Each remote location has a tab above the main editing window.
texts.text: Each remote location has a tab above the main editing window.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: If the report or lab is available in table format, CPRS will return data from all of the sites in a single table. The "facility" column indicates where the data in a particular row was collected. The table can be sorted by facility or by any other column heading (alphabetically, numerically, or by date) by clicking on the appropriate heading. Clicking on the heading again will sort the table in inverse order.
texts.text: If the report or lab is available in table format, CPRS will return data from all of the sites in a single table. The "facility" column indicates where the data in a particular row was collected. The table can be sorted by facility or by any other column heading (alphabetically, numerically, or by date) by clicking on the appropriate heading. Clicking on the heading again will sort the table in inverse order.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: To see detailed information about a particular item in the table, click on that item. If detailed information is available, it will be displayed in the bottom-half of the screen. To select multiple rows, press and hold the
texts.text: To see detailed information about a particular item in the table, click on that item. If detailed information is available, it will be displayed in the bottom-half of the screen. To select multiple rows, press and hold the

texts.label: text
texts.orig: or click the close box (the X button) in the upper right corner of the Window.
texts.text: or click the close box (the X button) in the upper right corner of the Window.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: CPRS		Computerized Patient Record System, the  package (in both GUI and character-based formats) that provides access to most components of the patient chart.
texts.text: CPRS		Computerized Patient Record System, the  package (in both GUI and character-based formats) that provides access to most components of the patient chart.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: product that can be viewed through CPRS, Health Summaries are components of patient information extracted from other  applications.
texts.text: product that can be viewed through CPRS, Health Summaries are components of patient information extracted from other  applications.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: PCE		Patient Care Encounter is a  program that is part of the Ambulatory Data Capture Project (ADCP) and also provides Clinical Reminders, which appear on Health summaries.
texts.text: PCE		Patient Care Encounter is a  program that is part of the Ambulatory Data Capture Project (ADCP) and also provides Clinical Reminders, which appear on Health summaries.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Reports		A component of CPRS that includes Health Summary, Action Profile, and other summarized reports of patient care.
texts.text: Reports		A component of CPRS that includes Health Summary, Action Profile, and other summarized reports of patient care.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Veterans Information Systems Technology Architecture, the new name for DHCP.
texts.text: Veterans Information Systems Technology Architecture, the new name for DHCP.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: DoD. See Department of Defense. See Department of Defense
texts.text: DoD. See Department of Defense. See Department of Defense

texts.text: GCPR. See Department of Defense. See Department of Defense
